news.summary: The stock market experienced a significant shift on Tuesday, reversing from substantial early gains due to the looming threat of massive Trump tariffs. This led to drops in the stock prices of Apple and Tesla. Dow Jones futures saw a decline as the market headed into the impact of the tariffs. The overall market reversal was described as 'ugly'.
news.headline: Dow Jones Futures fall as Trump tariffs loom; Apple and Tesla tumble in market reversal.

news.summary: A new study by Croí, the Heart & Stroke Charity in Ireland, and Global Heart Hub (GHH) uncovers significant disparities in knowledge of heart attack symptoms among women in Ireland. Heart disease ranks as a top cause of female mortality in the country, resulting in 1 out of every 4 women succumbing to cardiovascular issues. Despite this grave statistic, the survey sheds light on potential deficiencies in women's understanding of the dangers and symptoms, particularly in contrast to men. Close to the entire female population across Ireland may be lacking crucial awareness regarding these critical aspects of heart health.
news.headline: Low awareness of heart attack symptoms among Irish women poses danger. 1 in 4 women die from heart disease. Men better informed than women.
Brazil income reform commission president Pereira issues statement
Brazil expected income reform by House commission on week of April 21
US guidance on tariff goods from China effective April 9

news.summary: On April 8, 2025, in Washington, a federal court ruling was applauded by the National Press Club as it declared that discriminating against journalists based on their viewpoints is against the constitution. They stated that this decision is crucial in upholding accountability and protecting the public's access to information. This ruling marks a significant progress in ensuring journalistic freedom and promoting transparency.
news.headline: National Press Club applauds federal court ruling against discrimination, protecting journalists' rights. Key date: April 8, 2025.

news.summary: In Tipp City, Ohio, on April 8, 2025, Trophy Nut Company announced a recall of 143 cases of Heinen's 8.25 oz cans of Honey Roasted Peanuts. The reason behind the recall is the potential presence of cashews in the product. Consumers with a cashew allergy or high sensitivity to cashews are at risk of experiencing serious or life-threatening reactions.
news.headline: Trophy Nut Co. recalls 143 cases of Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts due to undeclared cashews - allergy risk.

news.summary: In La Jolla, California on April 8, 2025, MediciNova, Inc. disclosed that they have initiated enrollment in the NIH-backed Expanded Access Program trial to assess MN-166 (ibudilast) for patients with Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS). The biopharmaceutical company is listed on NASDAQ Global Market (NASDAQ:MNOV) and the Standard Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange (Code Number: 4875). The first patient has been enrolled in the trial.
news.headline: First ALS patient enrolled in NIH-funded EAP trial for MN-166 by MediciNova, NASDAQ:MNOV, to assess drug efficacy.
S. Korean Unemployment Rate Actual 2.9% (Forecast 2.8%, Previous 2.7%)
South Korea March seasonally adjusted jobless rate: 2.9%; est. 2.8%

news.summary: Several journalists have reported that certain Trump administration officials have refused to respond to questions due to their email signatures.
news.headline: Trump administration ignores journalists with pronouns in email signatures, refusing to respond to some questions.

news.summary: Following a community vote, Aavegotchi will be transferring from Polygon to Base. This migration decision will lead to the discontinuation of the game's layer-3 network, Geist.
news.headline: Aavegotchi to migrate from Polygon to Base after community vote, as Geist network set to shut down.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate has decreased to 4.2% in the United States. This is the lowest rate recorded since the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic. Additionally, the report shows that the economy added 235,000 jobs in the past month, exceeding economists' expectations. This increase in employment is seen as a positive sign for the country's economic recovery. The hospitality and leisure sectors experienced the most significant growth, with 74,000 new jobs added in these industries.Overall, the latest data suggests a promising outlook for the labor market as the economy continues to bounce back from the effects of the pandemic.
news.headline: Japanese stocks likely to continue falling after US volatility; investors on edge.

news.summary: In Brooklyn, New York, on April 08, 2025, Titan Acquisition Corp (Nasdaq: TACHU) disclosed the pricing for its initial public offering of 24,000,000 units valued at $10.00 each. These units will be traded on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol “TACHU” from April 9, 2025. Each unit includes one Class A ordinary share and half of a redeemable warrant. After the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and warrants are set to be listed on Nasdaq with the symbols “TACH” and “TACHW” respectively. Moreover, the underwriter holds a 45-day option to acquire up to 3,600,000 additional units from the Company to cover any over-allotments. The offering is anticipated to conclude on April 10, 2025, pending customary closing conditions.
news.headline: Titan Acquisition Corp priced its $240 million IPO with 24 million units at $10 each, to trade on Nasdaq as "TACHU" on April 9.

news.summary: In Houston, NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE: NRG) disclosed a quarterly dividend of $0.44 per share ($1.76 annually) for its common stock. Shareholders as of May 1, 2025, will receive the dividend on May 15, 2025. NRG is a Fortune 500 energy and home services firm committed to advancing a smarter, cleaner, and more connected future, driven by its workforce and dedication.
news.headline: NRG Energy, Inc. declared a quarterly dividend of $0.44 per share ($1.76 annually) payable on May 15, 2025. #energyfinance
Brazil Communications Minister Juscelino Filho resigns

news.summary: A recent report states that approximately 200 people have been confirmed dead due to the recent outbreak of a deadly virus. The infected cases have reached over 5,000 individuals, with more than 3,000 of them in critical condition. The virus has spread to over 10 countries, causing global concern among health officials. Authorities are working to contain the outbreak and provide medical assistance to those affected. The World Health Organization has issued a statement urging countries to take preventive measures to curb the spread of the virus. Efforts are being made to develop a vaccine to combat the deadly virus.
news.headline: IRS to provide immigrant tax information for criminal cases.

news.summary: The fourth generation of Llama, a highly popular open-source model in the AI community, has recently debuted amidst high anticipation and some controversy. This new iteration comes with significant promises, but has also sparked debates. We conducted a thorough test of the model to provide insights without the need for firsthand experience.
news.headline: Meta unveils highly anticipated Llama 4 models amidst hype. Widely used in AI, controversy surrounds its promises. We tested it for you.

news.summary: President Trump is set to announce new trade tariffs in an effort to address the U.S. trade deficit, which reached over $1.2 trillion in 2024. The tariffs could be as high as 20% on various imported goods, potentially raising average U.S. tariffs to levels not seen in decades. The announcement is expected to cause uncertainty in global supply chains and financial markets. Top sources of U.S. imports include Mexico, China, Canada, Germany, and the European Union. The address is scheduled for 4 p.m. and will be closely watched for details on diplomatic retaliation, corporate reactions, and the Federal Reserve's response. The U.S. imported $3.3 trillion in goods in 2024 and exported $2.06 trillion. In January and February 2025, monthly goods deficits hit record levels at $156 billion and $148 billion.
news.headline: Trump to announce 'Liberation Day' tariffs reaching up to 20% on imports to tackle U.S. trade deficit. Imported goods amounted to $3.3 trillion and exports stood at $2.06 trillion in 2024. Leading import sources are Mexico, China, Canada, Germany, and the European Union. Markets await Trump's 4 p.m. White House address for tariff details and potential global fallout.

news.summary: Wall Street saw continued gains on Wednesday, with investors showing resilience despite impending trade tariffs announcement by President Donald Trump. The president is expected to unveil tariffs of around 20% on various goods during his scheduled address at 4 p.m. ET, dubbed "Liberation Day." The S&P 500 rose by 0.8%, potentially marking its third positive session in a row. The Nasdaq 100 also made a strong rebound, climbing by 1.1%, while small-cap stocks, such as the Russell 2000, outperformed large caps by rising 1.5%. Most sectors showed growth, except defensive consumer staples, with consumer discretionary and tech sectors leading the way. Treasury yields increased, with the 30-year yield reaching 4.57%, up by 5 basis points. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wall Street rebounds ahead of tariff announcement, with S&P 500 up 0.8%, Nasdaq 100 rising 1.1%, and Bitcoin hitting $87,000.

news.summary: In early Friday trading in New York, Wall Street struggled as dip-buying was absent and fears of a global trade war escalated. Despite the U.S. economy adding 228,000 jobs in March—the strongest gain since December 2024, beating expectations of 135,000 per Bureau of Labor Statistics—the market remained in decline. Investors are worried that this positive employment report may not continue due to the expected economic impact of rising tariffs. The unemployment rate slightly increased to 4.2%, while wage growth was in line with predictions. Average hourly earnings saw a 0.3% monthly increase and a 3.8% yearly rise. The market reacted negatively to China imposing a retaliatory 34% tariff on U.S. goods, matching President Trump's recent actions. This move heightened concerns about an impending full-scale trade conflict.
news.headline: Nasdaq 100 in bear market, Dow in correction; U.S. added 228k jobs in March exceeding expectations. Tariff fears escalate.

news.summary: Matador Resources Co (NYSE: MTDR) saw a decline in shares after announcing the sale of its remaining Eagle Ford shale assets in South Texas. The assets were sold in transactions across La Salle, Karnes, and Atascosa Counties in South Texas. Matador's founder, chairman, and CEO, Joseph Wm. Foran, mentioned that the company will shift focus to developing its high-quality acreage in the northern Delaware Basin, recognized as the most productive basin in the U.S. Currently, Matador holds approximately 200,000 net acres in the Delaware Basin. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Matador Resources Co sells Eagle Ford assets in South Texas, plans buybacks, focusing on Delaware Basin growth.

news.summary: Analyst Zach Parham from J.P. Morgan recently discussed Magnolia Oil & Gas Corporation (NYSE: MGY) in anticipation of their upcoming earnings report on May 1st. Parham, who has a Neutral rating on the stock, updated his price target to $24, factoring in first-quarter commodity prices. He noted that Magnolia Oil & Gas is progressing well with its 2-rig/1-frac crew initiative and is dedicated to maintaining capex below 55% of EBITDA. Despite fluctuations in commodity prices, Parham predicts that capital expenditure will remain below 50% of EBITDA, largely due to the company's exposure to natural gas. More details are available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: J.P. Morgan analyst expects Magnolia Oil & Gas to maintain capex below 55% of EBITDA, with stock price forecast at $24.

news.summary: Investors on Wall Street were anticipating a turbulent and unpredictable approach from the re-elected President Trump, but were surprised by his strong dedication to the significant policy plans that were the cornerstone of his 2024 presidential campaign.
news.headline: Investors surprised by Trump 2.0's policy agenda; uncertainties loom with his commitment.

news.summary: The 10-year Treasury recently experienced its most successful week since August, with yields dropping below 4% due to a market shift towards safety. This led to investors moving away from risky assets and towards government bonds, causing Treasury prices to rise. The iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (NASDAQ: TLT) notably benefitted from this trend. The TLT has seen a 6.53% increase year-to-date and a 2.03% increase in the past month. Technically, the TLT is performing well, trading above its five-, 20- and 50-day moving averages.
news.headline: 10-year Treasury records best week since August as yields dip below 4%. iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (NASDAQ: TLT) up 6.53% year-to-date.

news.summary: After experiencing a significant decline throughout the day, the stock market saw a surge in selling activity in the final hours of trading on Friday. However, homebuilder stocks managed to bounce back amidst the volatile market conditions. The Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeted by 2,200 points while the tech-heavy Nasdaq entered bear territory. For live updates and coverage on these market developments, continue following the latest updates from Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Dow drops 2,200 points, Nasdaq enters bear territory in free fall market. Homebuilder stocks rebound.

news.summary: The global markets saw a significant drop in the first week of the new quarter following President Donald Trump's announcement of imposing new tariffs, which he referred to as "Liberation Day." The tariffs, which varied in rates based on the trading partner, were met with criticism from analysts who expressed concerns about the methodology and potential retaliation from other countries. The largest trade deficit partner, China, was hit with a massive 34% tariff, leading to Beijing announcing similar countermeasures by the week's end. This event marked the initiation of a trade war between the top two economies. Additionally, prominent trading partners faced tariffs as well: the European Union at 20%, Japan at 24%, and India at 26%. However, Canada and Mexico were exempted under the condition of complying with USMCA regulations. The unexpected severity of the situation...
news.headline: Trump's new tariffs cause S&P 500 to drop, worst weekly slump since 2020. Global markets plunge due to trade war fears. China hit with 34% tariff, EU faces 20%, Japan 24%, India 26%. Canada and Mexico spared with USMCA compliance.

news.summary: BofA Securities analyst Craig Siegenthaler has adjusted estimates and price forecasts for several broker stocks before earnings season. He warns of EPS risks related to cash sweep revenues, trading/margin loans, and AUM-based fees due to a potential equity market correction. Siegenthaler anticipates a slowdown in net new assets (NNA), especially in the retail channel compared to wealth management, with a modest decline projected for March. The analyst believes that risks from the correction will emerge more prominently in the second quarter of 2025. For Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE: SCHW), Siegenthaler predicts continued growth in net interest income (NII) and net new assets, with the latter estimated at 4.7%. Despite these positive trends, Siegenthaler maintains an Underperform rating on the company and lowers the price forecast from $74 to $66. EPS estimates for the first quarter of FY25 have been revised to $1.03, and for 2026 to $4.27. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: BofA analyst predicts slower growth for broker stocks due to market volatility. Revised forecasts show expected EPS risks and a NNA slowdown. Charles Schwab's NII forecasted to rise with net new asset growth of 4.7%. Underperform rating reiterated with price forecast lowered to $66. EPS estimates revised for first quarter FY25 to $1.03 and to $4.27 for 2026.

news.summary: When the President reveals his extensive range of new tariffs on Wednesday, the homebuilding industry, which is usually favored by the former real estate mogul, is expected to be particularly at risk.
news.headline: Trump to impose new tariffs, impacting vulnerable homebuilding industry.

news.summary: The Labor Department under the Trump administration has reduced funding for international labor rights specialists, causing concerns within the community. This decision is part of the administration's broader effort to cut spending on initiatives outside the country.
news.headline: Labor groups impacted by DOL's international cancellations due to Trump's money cuts beyond borders.

news.summary: Amid heightened global trade tensions, the latest data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics revealed a significant boost in nonfarm payrolls for March, with a surge of 228,000 jobs created. This figure exceeded economists' expectations of 135,000 jobs. The positive report has garnered attention from economists, who view it as a reassuring development amidst market volatility sparked by concerns over a potential trade war. Gregory Daco, the chief economist at EY, described the jobs report as "reassuring" in a post on X platform. However, Daco pointed out that certain indicators, such as wage growth and workforce participation, are showing signs of deceleration. In an interview with Yahoo Finance, Daco further elaborated on these trends. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Positive jobs report as nonfarm payrolls surge by 228,000 in March, exceeding forecasts of 135,000. Economists note slowing metrics like wage growth amidst global trade tensions.

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell notes cautious policy stance despite slowing economic momentum, emphasizing inflation concerns amidst higher tariffs that could complicate price stability. Speaking at a conference, Powell acknowledges trade uncertainty and regulatory changes increasing risk of slower growth and rising inflation. President Trump urges Powell to swiftly cut interest rates on Truth Social, citing decreased energy prices, interest rates, inflation, and a 69% drop in egg prices, while celebrating job growth success. Powell reaffirms Fed's dual mandate commitment but addresses challenges posed by rising tariffs, warning of larger-than-expected increases.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell cautious on rates due to tariffs impacting inflation. Trump urges rate cuts. Rising tariffs complicate path to price stability.

news.summary: Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, stated that the central bank's primary responsibility is to prevent a temporary rise in the price level from transforming into persistent inflation.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell cautions Trump's tariffs may raise inflation and slow growth. He aims to prevent a sustained rise in prices.

news.summary: BitMEX has recently launched NILUSDT perpetual swaps, providing traders with the opportunity to leverage up to 50 times. This addition broadens the range of trading choices available on the platform, catering to the needs of diverse market participants.
news.headline: Free from mentioning source: BitMEX debuts NILUSDT perpetual swaps with 50x leverage, expanding trading options on the platform.

news.summary: Houston-based energy company Nine Energy Service has disclosed the schedule for its first quarter earnings release and conference call for 2025. The company will reveal its financial results on a specified date and host a conference call to discuss these figures with investors and analysts. This update aims to provide stakeholders with valuable insights into the company's performance during this period.
news.headline: Nine Energy Service will release its first quarter 2025 earnings with a conference call in Houston.

news.summary: Effective March 25, 2025, BitMEX has implemented changes to the Mark Method for NILUSDTH25 and REDUSDTZ25 to Fair Price marking. This alteration aims to improve price accuracy on the platform by ensuring a fairer marking method for these specific trading pairs. These updates reflect BitMEX's commitment to enhancing the trading experience for its users and ensuring transparency in pricing mechanisms.
news.headline: BitMEX switches Mark Method to Fair Price for NILUSDTH25 and REDUSDTZ25 on March 25, 2025, improving accuracy.

news.summary: A Texas-based company, Excelerate Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EE), is set to unveil its first quarter 2025 results on May 7, 2025, after the U.S. financial markets close. Interested parties can access the earnings release and presentation on the investor section of Excelerate's website at www.excelerateenergy.com. Additionally, on May 8, 2025, the company's management will conduct a conference call for analysts and investors.
news.headline: Excelerate Energy (NYSE: EE) to release Q1 2025 results on May 7, 2025. Investors can access info on the company's website. Management to host conference call on May 8, 2025.

news.summary: In Atlanta, CIBC Innovation Banking has invested in SlicedHealth, Inc. through venture debt financing. The funding will support the company's platform development and employee expansion. SlicedHealth recently secured a Series A funding round led by Arthur Ventures in July 2024. The healthcare-focused company aims to address challenges faced by hospitals and providers who currently use manual methods and spreadsheets for payor contract modeling. This investment signifies a key step in enabling SlicedHealth to enhance its services and market presence.
news.headline: CIBC Innovation Banking invests in SlicedHealth with venture debt, aiding growth and adding jobs post Series A by Arthur Ventures.

news.summary: Wall Street is experiencing a relentless sell-off with U.S. equities facing the worst week since March 2020. The Dow Jones has entered correction territory and the Nasdaq 100 is in a bear market, dropping over 20% from its highs. Tech and semiconductor stocks are hit hard after China announces retaliatory tariffs of 34% on U.S. goods, matching rates from Trump's administration. The VIX index, measuring market fear, surged 35% to 40 levels, approaching the highest daily close since October 2020. Other nations threaten retaliation, heightening worries of a global trade war disrupting supply chains and impacting the economy. The iShares Semiconductor ETF has plummeted 16% this week, on course for its worst performance since September 2001.
news.headline: Wall Street faces worst week since March 2020; Dow Jones in correction, Nasdaq 100 in bear market. China tariffs hit tech, semiconductors hard leading to iShares Semiconductor ETF plunging 16%. VIX index spikes 35%. Global trade war fears mount.

news.summary: A close supporter of the president has raised concerns about the potential negative impact of trade barriers on American businesses and consumers. Despite these warnings, the president has not made any concrete efforts to dismantle these barriers.
news.headline: Senate Republicans, including Ted Cruz, criticize Trump's tariffs for potentially harming the US with no action to address it.

news.summary: The president's comprehensive tariff proposal has resulted in severe market disruption, potentially leading to a worldwide trade conflict.
news.headline: Trump stands firm on tariff policies despite market plunge; risks global trade war.

news.summary: In Philadelphia, Vital Start Health is seeking to raise $3 million in funding to expand its Pregnancy+ partnership program. This initiative aims to scale up the use of Virtual Reality technology for mental health support during pregnancy. The company plans to offer insurance coverage for these services, making them more accessible to a larger number of individuals.
news.headline: Vital Start Health secures $3M funding to enhance VR pregnancy support on Pregnancy+, aiming to boost mental health services.
Trump: very good meeting today with republican lawmakers

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the global economy is expected to grow by 5.3% in 2021, an increase from the previous forecast of 4.2%. This growth projection is largely attributed to the successful vaccine distribution and the significant fiscal support provided by governments worldwide. The recovery is anticipated to be led by advanced economies, which are expected to expand by 5.6% this year. However, emerging markets and developing economies are projected to grow by 6.3% collectively, with notable growth rates in countries like China and India. Despite these positive outlooks, there are concerns about rising inflation rates and the potential for uneven recovery across different regions and sectors. It is crucial for policymakers to carefully monitor these developments and implement appropriate measures to ensure sustainable and inclusive growth.
news.headline: Market responding to US tariffs under Trump. Join live Q&A session for insights. Visit Bloomberg for more details.

news.summary: PPG revealed the schedule for its first-quarter 2025 earnings report and teleconference call. The earnings release is set for Apr. 29 following the close of U.S. stock markets, while the teleconference will take place on Apr. 30 at 8 a.m. ET. Key participants include Tim Knavish, the chairman and CEO, Vince Morales, the senior VP and CFO, and Alex Lopez, the director of investor relations. Those interested can visit the website for dial-in registration.
news.headline: PPG to reveal first-quarter 2025 results on Apr. 29 in Pittsburgh; Tim Knavish, Vince Morales, and Alex Lopez to participate.
Markets respond to Trump's tariffs in live Q&A session. #tariffs #Trump #markets #Q&A

news.summary: In Toowoomba, Australia, the Arcadea Group has acquired Agdata Holdings, along with its flagship farm management software Phoenix. This software suite, which covers the complete Australian cropping and livestock production system, is designed to help producers manage their operations efficiently. The strategic acquisition by Arcadea Group, a long-term investor in high-quality vertical software businesses, will provide access to this integrated platform for streamlining agricultural data.
news.headline: Arcadea Group acquires Agdata Holdings, including Phoenix farm software, for Australian agriculture sector.

news.summary: Reservations are now available for the hotel and spa in anticipation of the grand opening of a highly awaited resort in East Hazel Crest, Illinois. Wind Creek Chicago Southland Casino & Resort revealed that the complete resort facilities, including the upscale hotel and spa, will be ready for guests come April 8, 2025.
news.headline: Wind Creek Chicago Southland to unveil hotel & spa on April 11, 2025. Bookings open, ahead of grand opening.

news.summary: Ellucian, a prominent provider of higher education technology solutions, recently recognized organizations within its Partner Network for their remarkable collaboration, innovation, and customer service. The prestigious acknowledgment was given out on April 8, 2025, in Reston, Virginia. Ellucian is known for being a leader in the field, offering cutting-edge technology solutions to institutions of higher education.
news.headline: Ellucian awards its partners for outstanding collaboration and innovation. RESTON, Va., April 8, 2025 /PRNewswire.

news.summary: RedStone, a blockchain oracle provider, has launched a push-based oracle on MegaETH to address latency issues in on-chain trading. The new oracle can update prices every 2.4 milliseconds, sourcing prices from centralized exchanges and delivering them to applications on MegaETH. The co-location approach minimizes latency by reducing physical distance delays. RedStone plans to integrate price feeds from decentralized exchanges in the future. There are currently 12 decentralized oracle networks on Ethereum, with a market capitalization of $10.2 billion. DeFi's total value locked nears $88 billion, with Ethereum leading at $47.8 billion, followed by Solana with $6.1 billion. The rise of DeFi has increased competition in the oracle market where Chainlink and Pyth Network are dominant players. Pyth recently surpassed Chainlink in a 30-day volume, offering a pull-based model suited for high-volume transactions.
news.headline: RedStone unveils push-based oracle on MegaETH to reduce trading latency, pushing new prices every 2.4 milliseconds. Plans to expand to other chains soon. Oracle market cap currently at $10.2B. DeFi TVL nears $88B, with Ethereum leading at $47.8B locked. Chainlink and Pyth Network are key players. Pyth surpasses Chainlink in 30-day volume at $36B. Financial innovation excites in crypto space.
